item.title: Bandai Namco Is Bringing My Hero Academia: All's Justice To Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
Arriving this September

Bandai Namco this week announced it would be releasing a Switch 2 version of MY HERO ACADEMIA: All's Justice.

The Nintendo release of this 3D battle action title, based on the "Final War" of the series, will arrive later this year on 4th September 2026. The game was previously released on PS5, Xbox Series X|S and Steam earlier this year.

"MY HERO ACADEMIA: ALL’S JUSTICE brings players into the climactic “Final War” of the series, delivering intense combat experiences inspired by the original work. Players can unleash powerful abilities by mastering the unique Quirks of both heroes and villains in high-impact battles faithful to the anime."

This release will also come with some features "exclusive" to the Switch 2 version, including online battle via GameShare.

A brand-new minigame mode, introducing a bunch of challenging activities featuring Class 1-A students, is being added as well. This will be accessible via a free update and includes a special collaboration with Pac-Man.

This title will be available as a standard edition, deluxe edition and ultimate edition. The Deluxe Edition includes the season pass and five additional playable characters (to be released post-launch) and the Ultimate Edition also includes the season pass. There will be a "package version" as well including a Game-Key Card.

Early purchases unlock the playable characters "Izuku Midoriya Rising" and "All For One Chaos", along with 30,000 hero coins (in-game currency for items and minigames).

Would you be interested in this game on the Switch 2? Have you watched the anime or read the manga? Let us know in the comments.

[source en.bandainamcoent.eu]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: CDL Major 4 Tournament Guide—Schedule, Format, and Teams
item.content:encoded:
The Call of Duty League Major 4 tournament is the final stop before COD Champs, which means it’s one of the most important events of the CDL 2026 schedule. It’s being hosted in Paris by the Gentle Mates, and by sheer ticket sales alone, it’s set to be the biggest Call of Duty League Major in history.

In this guide, you’ll find everything you need to know about CDL Major 4, including the schedule, format breakdown, teams competing, and even some Major 4 predictions to help you with Call of Duty esports betting.

Read on to learn everything possible about CDL Major 4!

CDL Major 4 2026 Schedule and Format

CDL Major 4 starts on June 26th and ends on June 28th, which means it’s your traditional, short-form Call of Duty tournament. Over those three days, teams will compete in a double-elimination bracket event, which every COD esports fan should be familiar with.

It’s a best-of-five bracket, except for the grand final, which will be a best-of-seven series.

Here are the first matches for CDL Major 4 on Friday, June 26th:

  • LA Thieves v TBC
  • Paris Gentle Mates v TBC
  • Riyadh Falcons v TBC
  • OpTic Texas v TBC

At the time of writing, the final qualifying matches were yet to be played, which meant that not all teams had been arranged in their starting position for Major 4.

In terms of prize money, here’s the split for Major 4:

  • 1st Place: $150,000 & 100 CDL Points
  • 2nd Place: $90,000 & 75 CDL Points
  • 3rd Place: $50,000 & 60 CDL Points
  • 4th Place: $30,000 & 45 CDL Points
  • 5th – 6th Place: $15,000 & 30 CDL Points
  • 7th – 8th Place: $7,500 & 15 CDL Points
  • 9th – 12th Place: $0 and 0 CDL Points
  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

COD League Major 4 Teams

Here are all the teams heading to Paris for the CDL Major 4 event, listed by their regular season standings rank at the time of writing:

  • OpTic Texas
  • LA Thieves
  • FaZe Vegas
  • Paris Gentle Mates
  • Toronto KOI
  • Riyadh Falcons
  • Miami Heretics
  • G2 Minnesota
  • Carolina Royal Ravens
  • Boston Breach
  • Vancouver Surge
  • Cloud9 New York

How to Watch CDL Major 4

You can watch the fourth Major of the season via the usual channels:

  • COD League on YouTube
  • COD League on Twitch

CDL Major 4 2026 Predictions

The LA Thieves enter Major 4 as the number-one seed and top-rated team to win the trophy, again. At Major 3, LAT beat OpTic Texas in a dramatic grand final to secure the $150,000 prize, a victory that came almost an entire year after the organization’s last major victory and followed several miserable defeats at the hands of all comers.

Now, nothing stands in the way of LAT and victory going into Major 4 in Paris, except for perhaps the Riyadh Falcons, on top form in the last few weeks. OpTic Texas has been flagging a bit recently, but there’s still no doubting the team’s efficacy in the CDL right now.

Looking at sheer standings alone, the predictions would be LAT to win, and either OpTic Texas or Riyadh Falcons to make it to the grand final, but everyone knows that you can discredit the other challengers in the lineup.

For instance, Paris Gentle Mates sits on home turf, and that can be powerful for an organization. The squad has had a fairly paltry year so far since winning at Major 1, winding up in fifth place twice beyond that first win of the season. Could we see Paris repeat the opener for the 2026 CDL season and make it big in the first-ever CDL Major in Paris?

If we’re talking about Call of Duty betting, my money would be on LAT to pull off a back-to-back Major win, going into this year’s Call of Duty League Championship in Las Vegas with white-hot momentum.

Are you going to be watching the Call of Duty League Major 4 tournament? Let us know your predictions on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, learn what’s wrong with the COD League, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1106)—Answers for Sunday, June 21
item.content:encoded:
It’s time to add another notch onto that NYT Connections streak. In this guide, you’ll find the NYT Connections hints and solutions you need to power through the puzzle and come out the other side without too much fuss. If you’re stuck on today’s NYT Connections challenge, simply refer to the hints below!

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections answers you need!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (June 21)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • BARBADOS
  • COMMUNITY
  • DIGGITY
  • DISSECT
  • DRIZZLE
  • FLOORS
  • FRIENDS
  • RAIN
  • ROCKS
  • SCRUBS
  • SHOWERS
  • SLAPDASH
  • SPRINKLES
  • STUNS
  • SURPRISES
  • WINGS

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words used to describe precipitation
  • Green – Words used to describe ‘bowling over’ someone
  • Blue – Names of popular NBC sitcoms
  • Purple – Words that start with another word for an insult

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, June 21!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, June 21 (#1106)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – DRIZZLE, RAIN, SHOWERS, SPRINKLES
  • Green – FLOORS, ROCKS, STUNS, SURPRISES
  • Blue – COMMUNITY, FRIENDS, SCRUBS, WINGS
  • Purple – BARBADOS, DIGGITY, DISSET, SLAPDASH

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

item.title: Halo: Campaign Evolved PS5 split-screen issue clarified
item.content:encoded:
Halo: Campaign Evolved is little more than a month away from its highly anticipated multi-platform launch, though its developers have been catching some flak following a recent community Q&A.

Halo Studios (formerly 343 Industries) recently published a blog that answered a slate of questions, initially creating some confusion in regards to how the PS5 version will handle split-screen co-op. Originally, Halo Studios had stated that in order to access this feature, both players would need to link their PlayStation Network profile to a Microsoft account while also needing an active PlayStation Plus subscription.

In an updated version of the blog, it’s been confirmed that a PlayStation Plus subscription is only required if playing online. Still, players will need to jump through the other aforementioned hoops in order to play Halo: Campaign Evolved locally with a friend via split-screen, rather than simply connect a second controller. Presumably, this will allow both players to keep their progress and any unlocked trophies they both earn.

While the PS5 split-screen clarification will come as a relief, some Halo fans aren’t happy with other details gleaned from the Campaign Evolved Q&A. Specifically, the lack of a dedicated photo mode comes as a disappointing omission given how widespread this feature is in modern AAA games, not to mention Halo’s long legacy of giving players tools to create their own fan-made media. According to Halo Studios, “there are no current plans for a Photo Mode or Theater Mode in Halo: Campaign Evolved.”

Also, despite the number of newly introduced customisation options available, you won’t get to see them in cutscenes. Instead, Master Chief will revert to his vanilla appearance “to ensure that the narrative remains as faithful to the original game as possible”. Hopefully, we’ll get the option to toggle this feature in a post-launch update.

When Halo: Campaign Evolved launches on July 28th, 2026, it will signal a landmark moment for the former flagship Xbox franchise as it makes its PlayStation debut, almost 25 years after the sci-fi shooter helped spearhead Microsoft’s push into the console market.

As the name suggests, this is a full-on remake of the Halo: Combat Evolved single player campaign. While it would have been nice to see some additional multiplayer modes added, Microsoft probably wants to avoid fracturing its existing fanbase which is already spread across Halo: Infinite and Halo: The Master Chief Collection.

Source: Halo Waypoint



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/21/halo-campaign-evolved-ps5-split-screen-playstation-plus/

item.title: Murder Duels Codes (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox Murder Duels monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including Coins and much more.

Murder Duels is a competitive Roblox experience where engage in fast-paced duels using knives, revolvers, and throwing knives, competing in various team configurations from solo 1v1 matches to coordinated 4v4 battles. The game uses a best-of-9-round format, leading to extended matches in which players must consistently outperform their opponents to claim victory.

Combat in Murder Duels revolves around mastering three distinct weapon types, each offering unique tactical advantages. Beyond basic weaponry, the game features an ability system that adds strategic depth to encounter. Players can customize their loadouts with cosmetic weapon skins obtained through cases, allowing them to personalize their arsenal while climbing the competitive ladder.

Since it’s launch, Murder Duels has built a dedicated competitive community, accumulating over 24 million visits. While the core gameplay loop of dueling and improving provides continuous progression opportunities, redeem codes offer players additional resources like weapon cases, giving them faster access to cosmetic customization options without spending Robux.

Active Murder Duels Codes

  • MATCHMAKINGDELAY: Free Rewards
  • 20KLIKES: Recruit Case
  • RELEASE: 1000 Coins

Expired Murder Duels Codes

  • 1KLIKES
  • 10KLIKES

How To Redeem Murder Duels Codes

Redeeming codes in Murder Duels is a little different as compared to other Roblox games. Here’s how players can claim their freebies:

  1. Boot up and play Murder Duels in Roblox
  2. Reach level 5 in the game and then join the Breakfast Roblox Group
  3. After that, find the shop icon on the bottom of the screen
  4. Scroll to the codes section and click on it
  5. Type or paste an active code in the “Enter Code Here” box
  6. Click on redeem to claim them.

Where To Find More Murder Duels Codes

Since Murder Duels doesn’t have an in-game notification system for code releases, you’ll need to stay plugged into where the developers actually communicate. Breakfast’s Discord server is the primary hub where codes get announced first, usually buried in update posts or dropped during community events. Hanging out in the server gives you the added benefit of learning from veteran players who often share codes they’ve discovered on Twitter or YouTube before those codes make the rounds everywhere else.

Social platforms are where developers like to reward the community during big moments. You’ll see codes pop up when Murder Duels crosses player milestones, launches new game modes, or rolls out weapon updates. These releases are completely unpredictable—there’s no schedule, no advance notice, and codes can vanish without warning once they hit their redemption cap or time limit. Missing a 24-hour code because you weren’t scrolling at the right moment is frustrating.

Thus the best approach is this page. Bookmark this page and swing by whenever you’re jumping into Murder Duels. We update the code list the moment new ones are confirmed active, and we scrub expired codes immediately so you’re not wasting time on duds.

item.title: Cavity Crushers Codes (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox Cavity Crushers monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including  and much more.

Cavity Crushers throws players into an unexpectedly creative premise—defending the human mouth from relentless bacterial invasions using an army of sentient teeth. This Roblox tower defense experience, developed by Simple and Fiya Games, transforms dental hygiene into an engaging strategic battlefield where players must think tactically to survive increasingly aggressive waves of microscopic enemies.

The core gameplay revolves around collecting and deploying various teeth units, each with distinct defensive capabilities and attack patterns. Players earn Enamels—the game’s primary currency—by successfully repelling bacterial assaults, which can then be invested in capsules that unlock more powerful dental defenders. The strategic depth comes from positioning your tooth army effectively and continuously upgrading both your teeth and toothbrushes to handle tougher enemy waves.

Rare and secret teeth offer unique abilities that can turn the tide of battle, encouraging players to experiment with different defensive compositions. Since its release, Cavity Crushers has carved out its niche in the Roblox tower defense genre with its humorous concept and accessible gameplay loop.

Active Cavity Crushers Codes

  • 1MVISITS: 75 Pass points
  • 250k: Infected Mutated Tooth
  • Release: 500 Coins

Expired Cavity Crushers Codes

As of the writing of this article, there are no expired Cavity Crushers Codes.

How To Redeem Cavity Crushers Codes

Redeem codes in Cavity Crushers is rather simple, as it is for most Roblox games. Simply follow these steps to redeem any active Cavity Crushers codes.

  1. Boot Cavity Crushers in Roblox
  2. Look to the left side of the screen and find the Shop icon
  3. Scroll all the way to the bottom of the menu to the code entry box
  4. Type or paste an active code into the text box
  5. Click on ‘redeem’ to claim the freebie

Where To Find More Cavity Crushers Codes

Staying updated on new Cavity Crushers codes requires knowing where the developers actually communicate with their community. The game’s official Discord server is the primary hub where Simple and Fiya Games share fresh redemption codes, often bundled with announcements about new teeth units, gameplay adjustments, and upcoming features. Joining the Discord gives you direct access to the source while connecting you with fellow players who share code discoveries and strategic tips for maximizing your dental defense setup.

Social media platforms offer supplementary code drops, particularly when Cavity Crushers reaches community milestones. The developers have been known to celebrate achievements like hitting visitor thresholds or successful update launches by distributing time-sensitive codes across their social channels. These codes typically appear during special occasions, whether it’s the introduction of powerful new tooth types, seasonal events, or when the game crosses significant engagement markers.

Since these releases are unpredictable and codes can expire without advance notice, missing a 24-hour window means missing out on exclusive rewards like rare teeth or substantial currency boosts. 

That said, there’s a simpler approach than juggling multiple platforms. Save this guide in your browser and check back instead of trying to monitor every source yourself. This page is refreshed as soon as fresh codes go live, and any codes that have expired are taken out quickly so you always see only the ones that still work.

item.title: Lucky Block Rush Codes (June 2026)
item.content:encoded:
Use these Roblox Lucky Block Rush monthly redeem codes to get your hands on plenty of free rewards, including Potions and much more.

Lucky Block Rush is an engaging Roblox experience that throws players into a luck-based combat adventure where defeating enemies and opening lucky blocks go hand in hand. Players must fight increasingly powerful foes to boost their luck stat, with each victory rewarding them with a Lucky Block containing valuable Brainrot; collectible items that generate passive income even when offline.

The core gameplay loop revolves around strategic combat and resource management. As players defeat enemies, they unlock different types of Brainrots with varying rarities, each contributing to their overall progression. The game’s appeal lies in its blend of active battling and idle mechanics, allowing players to customize their approach based on how much time they want to invest in grinding versus collecting passive rewards.

Since it’s release, Lucky Block Rush has carved out its niche in the Roblox ecosystem by combining the addictive nature of gacha-style collection with action-oriented gameplay.

item.title: Stranger Than Heaven Second-Take Preview: Stranger Than Heaven’s Combat Seems Harder Than Hell, and that’s a Good Thing
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been a fan of the Yakuza or Like a Dragon series since the very beginning. As a magazine writer I was lucky enough to visit Sega’s headquarters in Tokyo during the development of the original PlayStation 2 title way back in 2005, and in my years at IGN I’ve reviewed a number of the remakes, sequels, and spin-offs of RGG Studio’s long-running crime series. At this point I’ve invested hundreds of hours into beating the absolute snot out of Japanese mobsters, either via the button-mashing combo-based style of the original adventures or the turn-based RPG form that’s become core to the most recent mainline entries. Thus I feel pretty well-equipped to handle anything that RGG Studio can throw my way at this point, which is why I was so shocked that the new combat system in upcoming series prequel Stranger Than Heaven so completely kicked my arse.

At a recent BiliBili Game First Look event in Shanghai, I got some brief hands-on time with a brawling-based demo for Stranger Than Heaven. (It was the same demo that my colleague Michael Higham recently played, and you can read his excellent preview here.) My first attempts at Stranger Than Heaven’s all-new street fighting style felt harder than hell, but after slowly getting to grips with it over the course of my 30-minute demo I began to feel confident that this is exactly the sort of substantial shift that the Like a Dragon fighting formula has needed for quite a while now.

To be clear, I’m not suggesting that RGG Studio needs to scrap the turn-based combat system that it introduced in Yakuza: Like a Dragon and later evolved in Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth. The novelty of that JRPG-style system is yet to wear off for me, largely because it allows for so many hilarious special moves and a more strategic team-based dynamic in each scrap. I’m talking about the more traditional beat ‘em up system that has effectively been offloaded onto the series’ spin-offs in recent years. RGG Studio may have introduced a briefcase-load of James Bond gadgets in Like a Dragon Gaiden, or cutlasses and flintlocks in Pirate Yakuza, but the actual fighting mechanics of those combo-based systems has remained unaltered for the most part. Even though I still enjoy them, I can’t pretend that they don’t feel a bit too familiar at this point.

Stranger Than Heaven’s heavily overhauled scrapping, then, feels like the kick in the pants followed by a violent uppercut to the jaw that Like a Dragon’s aging fisticuffs has been crying out for. Forget about chugging in-game energy drinks to pump up your heat gauge and then button-mashing hordes of street thugs into oblivion like in previous Kazama Kiryu-led adventures, because Stranger Than Heaven’s gangs are liable to quickly kick you to the curb if you don’t have your wits about you. The new setup that uses the controller’s four shoulder buttons to effectively puppeteer the left and right fists of protagonist Makoto Daito sure takes some getting used to, both in terms of smoothly alternating his punches but also anticipating the direction of incoming attacks and parrying them effectively, but after finally falling into a rhythm with it after my first few failed attempts it felt incredibly satisfying to pull off. I felt like I had to lock-in for absolutely every exchange in every combat encounter, which meant that my eventual victories felt far more earned than the lazy bicycle-swinging beatdowns of previous Yakuza and Like a Dragon installments.

BiliBili Game First Look Event Photos

Though its learning curve is initially steep, it ultimately feels like the most grounded form of street fighting that RGG Studio has ever designed. In fact, its more rough and tumble realism reminded me of the melee system in 007 First Light, only it felt like I had far more direct control over the moves I was pulling off thanks to the dedicated right and left attack setup. For example, as I wielded a large crowbar in my right hand while tackling a gang of six or so assailants, one of the goons grabbed hold of the end of my weapon and attempted to wrench it out of my grasp. To my surprise, while I was locked in a temporary tug-o-war I still had full control of Makoto’s left hand, which allowed me to throw a few jabs at a second attacker attempting to blindside me. I then grabbed him by the scruff of the neck and knocked his skull into the first thug, taking them both out and allowing me to resume my crowbar-based bullying.

[Stranger Than Heaven's] more rough and tumble realism reminded me of the melee system in 007 First Light, only it felt like I had far more direct control over the moves I was pulling off.“

Having said that, Stranger Than Heaven’s combat is not without its issues, at least in its current state. I found that the soft lock-on that aligns Makoto with each opponent would occasionally slip, sending me swinging wildly in the wrong direction and leaving me vulnerable to attacks from the rear. Also, while I enjoyed the generally weighty feel to each and every blow I landed, some of the charged up attacks with heavier weapons seemed a touch too sluggish for my tastes. Of course, with Stranger Than Heaven not due for release until early next year, there’s still time for RGG Studio to tighten things up.

Still my overall impression of Stranger Than Heaven’s more challenging brawling system is a positive one, and its dramatic shakeup leaves me feeling optimistic that combat won’t be the only area of the adventure where the developers are taking a big swing. I'm hopeful that this will present the perfect opportunity for RGG Studio to hit the reset button on all aspects of its long established Like a Dragon template, from mission design to the interactive nature of its world and the methods we use to explore it. For example, the audio-recording feature that allows Makoto to record sound samples from the world around him and then convert them into musical compositions certainly sounds cool on paper, and already seems like it could be far more involved than the simple rhythm-based button-tapping of the karaoke mini-game that’s been recycled in every Yakuza and Like a Dragon release since Yakuza 3.

Tristan Ogilvie's Top 10 Like a Dragon/Yakuza Games

It's Japandemonium!

See All

I also wonder what other new diversions there could be, given that the story’s 1915 to 1965 setting presumably prohibits the use of the usual modern side activities like Club Sega’s and Master System cartridges found in other Like a Dragon stories. We already know there’ll be arm wrestling mini-games, but could there be old school pachinko parlours as well? And given that the events of Stranger Than Heaven span the course of five decades, will those side activities evolve with each leap forward in time? Not to mention the change in each of the five city settings. You’d think that by the time the story arrives in 1965 Shinjuku, we should be able to try out virtual versions of the electromechanical amusements that Sega famously pioneered during that decade.

I’m desperately keen to learn more, because so far I’ve been pretty excited by everything that RGG Studio has revealed. Well, I mean except for the Tupac thing, of course. Why on earth is Tupac in Stranger Than Heaven? Well, much like the gang of burly thugs I encountered in my hands-on, it beats the heck out of me. Sure, it makes sense that Snoop Dogg is in the game, because Snoop Dogg is in basically everything at this point from televised singing contests to carrying the torch at the last Olympic Games. But having the late great Tupac resurrected in a 2027 videogame for no clear reason just feels a bit off to me. Then again, I’m generally opposed to digital zombies in any modern media, no matter who signs off on it. I can’t watch the CGI’d Peter Cushing reprise his role as Moff Tarkin in Star Wars: Rogue One without sensing a great disturbance in the Force, for example.

However, that doesn’t prevent me from rewatching Rogue One from time to time, and nor will the somewhat baffling inclusion of Tupac deter me from lunging fists-first into Stranger Than Heaven when it arrives in January 2027. Despite the fact its story takes place in the same fictional Japan as the Like a Dragon saga, Stranger Than Heaven feels as though it's determined to establish its own identity – at least if its fresh-feeling and fiercely challenging fighting system is any indication. Here’s hoping that in all facets, Stranger Than Heaven finds the team at RGG Studio performing stronger than ever, and that this ends up being something much more than just a ye olde Yakuza.

Tristan Ogilvie is a Senior Video Editor at IGN's Sydney office. He attended the recent BiliBili Game First Look event as a guest of the organisers.



https://www.ign.com/articles/stranger-than-heaven-second-take-preview-stranger-than-heavens-combat-seems-harder-than-hell-and-thats-a-good-thing

item.title: Gallery: Nintendo Shares Multiple Screenshots Of Nintendo Switch Sports Resort
item.content:encoded:
100 Pin Bowling returns

This October, Wuhu Island is returning in Nintendo Switch Sports Resort. This new game for the Switch 2 features 12 different sports and "intuitive motion controls", where you'll make use of the Joy-Con 2 as bows, paddles and more.

The 12 sports include Boxing, Table Tennis, Archery, Tennis, Volleyball, Bowling, Basketball, Golf, Thumb Wrestling, Skateboarding, Power Cruising, and Prop Plane.

Apart from the recent trailer, Nintendo also shared some screenshots alongside the announcement, showcasing the different sports. If you didn't already catch it, Nintendo's official Japanese website also confirms the return 100 pin bowling (thanks, Nintendo Everything).

While we're at it, here is a look at the many other sports in this upcoming release. The title will support 1-4 players locally and online multiplayer, and there's also a shot of the game's splitscreen multiplayer.

Will you be getting this game when it launches for the Switch 2 on 22nd October 2026? Let us know in the comments.

[source nintendo.com, via nintendoeverything.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.
